sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 33

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 33:1
items.html: (to the question) For the corrections made in the Ibur [conception], Yenika [suckling], and Mochin of ZA [Zeir Anpin] — although they were made while the Nukva was not yet in existence, since her existence begins after the Gadlut of ZA — are nevertheless considered the discernments of the roots of correction for the Nukva. For afterwards, when ZA comes to correct the Nukva, he corrects her out of the discernments of his own times of Katnut: when he imparts Mochin of Ibur to her, he awakens the discernment of the Masach [screen] and all the corrections he had at the time when he himself was in Ibur; likewise, when he imparts Mochin of Yenika to her, he awakens the discernments of the Masach and the corrections he had at the time of Yenika, and imparts them to the Nukva; and likewise in Gadlut. For this reason, all the corrections ZA had in the order of his own Ibur, Yenika, and Gadlut are considered roots for the correction of the Ibur, Yenika, and Gadlut of the Nukva. (p. 772, beginning "And after")
